content.question: What is the true prevalence rate of neurodevelopmental and learning conditions in Hungary's school-age population, and how much of the measured 1.6-fold increase in identification over 20 years is attributable to true prevalence rise, definitional change, diagnostic-practice improvement, or incentive-driven over-identification?
content.reduction_path: Commission a population-based prevalence study using harmonized diagnostic instruments on a representative cohort (e.g., ages 6–16) to establish a baseline comparable to international datasets; conduct a parallel historical audit of definitional changes and incentive structures in SEN classification policy from 2003–2025 to quantify the contribution of each mechanism.
